title: Present continuous, will, be going to, present simple
description: Learn how to use the present continuous, will, be going to, and present simple to talk about future time.

topics.title: Present continuous
topics.description: Used for future arrangements: plans that have already been made, usually with another person, often including a time or place.

uses.example: We're driving to Berlin this weekend.

notes.content: For the full statement, negative and question forms, see Unit 1.
Things we want to do in the future but have not arranged are called "intentions". We do not use the present continuous for intentions. We use be going to instead.
examples: Incorrect: I'm becoming an explorer when I grow up.
Correct: I'm going to become an explorer when I grow up.

notes.content: We do not use the present continuous for predictions. We use will or be going to instead.
examples: Incorrect: Do you think you're enjoying your trip to Berlin next week?
Correct: Do you think you'll enjoy your trip to Berlin next week?

topics.title: Will
topics.description: Used for facts about the future, predictions, offers and requests, and decisions made at the moment of speaking.

columns: I / you / he / she / it / we / they

uses.example: The new airport will be the biggest in Europe.
You'll have a great time in the Bahamas.
We'll help you get ready for your holiday.
I know! I'll go to China this summer.

notes.content: With offers which are questions, we use Shall with I and we.
examples: Shall I drive you to the airport?

notes.content: We do not use will for arrangements.
examples: Incorrect: We'll visit my grandma this weekend.
Correct: We're visiting my grandma this weekend.

topics.title: Be going to
topics.description: Used for intentions, predictions often made with evidence we can see now, and facts about the future.

rows.label: Negative
cells: I am not ('m not) going to travel ...
He/She/It is not (isn't / 's not) going to travel ...
You/We/They are not (aren't / 're not) going to travel ...

uses.example: I'm going to become an explorer when I grow up.

uses.usage: Predictions, often with evidence we can see
uses.example: It's going to rain, so take an umbrella.
The new airport is going to be the biggest in Europe.

topics.title: Present simple
topics.description: Used for fixed timetables and schedules, such as transport and events.

notes.content: For the full statement, negative and question forms, see Unit 1.
